channel.title: HSJ - Latest news
channel.description: Custom RSS feed for HSJ latest news

item.title: Hospital trust ‘deeply sorry’ for harm to dozens of children
item.description: At least 40 children suffered harm – with over 20 cases classed as “moderate or severe” – due to delays while receiving care from a hospital’s audiology department, HSJ can reveal.

item.title: Charity director to chair trust
item.description: An acute trust will be chaired by a charity leader who grew up locally.

item.title: Health minister resigns
item.description: Zubir Ahmed has become the first health minister to quit in the current batch of resignations, saying his decision was guided by surgical principles.

item.title: CEO: ICB must take ‘urgent action on shameful situation’
item.description: The boss of a trust where a child recently spent over two months in A&E has urged other local system leaders to take “urgent action” to help resolve the “shameful situation” concerning vulnerable children.

item.title: Three times more GPs leaving some ICBs
item.description: Some systems saw fully qualified GPs leave their roles at three times the rate of other patches last year, according to new figures.

item.title: Trust’s £360k overtime bill for handful of staff ‘not sustainable’
item.description: A trust has said it paid £358,000 in overtime to just eight band 6 and 7 staff members to keep its pathology service running amid multiple strikes last year, in an arrangement the CEO warned was “not sustainable”.

item.title: Cash bailouts hit £1.1bn despite falling deficit
item.description: Cash bailouts to keep trust services running have risen – despite NHS England bosses insisting provider finances have improved, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Exclusive: Govt debating jobs freeze on admin roles which can be done by AI
item.description: The government is considering recommending that trusts “embrace automation” by freezing recruitment and decommissioning training places, HSJ has learned.

item.title: Region declares bid to eliminate A&E mental health waits
item.description: London trusts are launching a “blueprint” for service change that they hope will stop people in mental health crisis needing to attend A&E.

item.title: Three trusts in 'intensive recovery' confirm CEOs
item.description: A trust group has confirmed that its interim chief executive will take on the job permanently, just weeks after governors blocked an attempt to hire her without an open recruitment exercise.

item.title: Trust upgraded despite staff reports of discrimination and fear
item.description: A large acute trust has had its leadership rating upgraded from “inadequate”, despite serious concerns including allegations that a board member made “divisive and discriminatory remarks” about a Ramadan initiative.

item.title: ICB drafts in neighbour to review restructure
item.description: An integrated care board that was forced to reopen its consultation on staff cuts has called in a neighbour to review its plans.

item.title: US equity firm buys biggest supplier of GP IT
item.description: An American private equity firm has bought the company supplying more than half of England’s primary care IT systems.

item.title: NHSE tech funding priorities revealed
item.description: Regions are prioritising investing in ambient voice technology and cyber security over other technology this year, HSJ understands.

item.title: ‘Concerning’ levels of therapy still carried out by unaccredited staff
item.description: Tens of thousands of therapy sessions are still being carried out by unaccredited practitioners in the NHS, data suggests – nearly four years after the deadline to stamp this out.

item.title: Streeting intervenes over energy price shock
item.description: The health secretary has urged hospital chief executives to sign up to a national energy procurement scheme to cushion themselves from energy price shocks caused by the war in Iran.

item.title: Failing private service faces NHS takeover
item.description: A leading NHS provider is considering taking over the running of services at a private hospital at the centre of a police probe.

item.title: Exclusive: No 10 sets new GP access target
item.description: A new target for improving patients’ experience of making GP appointments is among three top NHS priorities identified by the prime minister for this year, HSJ has learned.

item.title: NHSE locks down ‘open source’ code over Mythos AI fears
item.description: NHS England is restricting access to open source code after researchers found that the Mythos AI model could expose “pretty severe” vulnerabilities in commonly used software.

item.title: Mackey’s heir named number one trust CEO
item.description: The top two trust chief executives in the 12th edition of HSJ’s annual ranking of the most respected provider CEOs have taken very different routes to the summit.
